tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship is urgent and self-contained, requiring three focused hours to secure significant funding. Attending the fun event now risks delaying this critical task until tomorrow.
Starting the scholarship application now builds a buffer against potential delays, significantly reducing Thursday's stress without sacrificing future leisure options.

Despite two hours of effort, the scholarship application remains largely unfinished with only three days left. I have lost half my free time without securing a significant buffer against the deadline.
I made partial progress on the scholarship but missed a casual leisure opportunity, leaving me with 1.5 hours of work remaining and lingering stress.
The 1.5-hour session pushed scholarship progress to 60%, but with only 3 hours needed total and a deadline in 3 days, I still face significant remaining work.
My 1.5-hour session increased scholarship progress to 66%, but I still need roughly 1.2 hours of focused work to finish the application before the deadline in three days.
My scholarship progress reached 72% after another hour and a half of work, leaving me with about 1.8 hours remaining for the deadline in three days.
After working another hour, my scholarship application is 76% complete, but I still need roughly 0.8 hours to finish the remaining tasks before the deadline.
After one hour of work, my scholarship application is now 80% complete. I have made steady progress but still need to finish the remaining tasks within the next three days.
After 8 hours of sleep, my energy is fully restored at 9.6 with no sleep debt, positioning me to tackle the remaining scholarship tasks efficiently.
The scholarship application is now fully complete after two hours of focused work, eliminating the remaining 0.8 hours needed and meeting the three-day deadline.
The scholarship application is now fully complete, meeting the three-day deadline with one hour of work remaining. I can consider this task finished and focus on other priorities.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
